name: Quality Tier 2 — single table, scalar aggregations (AVG/SUM/MIN/MAX)
description: Single-table aggregations, scalar return. Each scenario carries the expected scalar (numeric, compared with 0.1%-relative tolerance). Tests aggregation routing, optional filter-with-aggregate, and the structural 'single-row aggregate' rule (empty select + aggregations only).

Scenarios.Question: what is the average bill amount
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: AVG(Bills.TotalAmount), single-row aggregate, empty select, no GROUP BY
Scenarios.Notes: Pure AVG. Verifies the 'select MUST be empty' rule for single-row aggregates.

Scenarios.Question: total amount across all bills
Scenarios.Notes: SUM not COUNT. 'total amount' must route to SUM(TotalAmount), not COUNT(*).

Scenarios.Question: highest bill amount ever issued
Scenarios.Notes: MAX on a numeric column. Verifies 'highest X' synonym routing to MAX.

Scenarios.Question: average meter reading value
Scenarios.Notes: Same shape as QT2-AVG-BILL but a different root entity. Cross-entity sanity check.

Scenarios.Question: average bill amount for paid bills
Scenarios.Notes: AVG + status filter. Critical: must keep select EMPTY even with a filter. This is the rule that broke BL-MS-001 originally.

Scenarios.Question: total amount of overdue bills
Scenarios.Notes: SUM + status filter on a smaller-value subset (~3% of bills, so a wrong status filter would be obvious).
